task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris educational software main menu or interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the Fifteen Puzzle activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Fifteen Puzzle activity (a 4x4 grid for sliding tiles) is open and visible within the GCompris application.

The Fifteen Puzzle tiles are arranged in perfect numerical order (1 to 15, reading left-to-right, top-to-bottom) with the empty space in the bottom right corner. A success animation or indicator may also be visible.

task_completion.description: Use a tool to capture a screenshot of the solved puzzle
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot utility (e.g., GNOME screenshot, Flameshot) is active, or a terminal command for taking a screenshot is visible while the solved puzzle is on screen.

task_completion.description: Save the screenshot to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal window explicitly shows the screenshot being saved as 'fifteen_puzzle_solved.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris application
integrity.visual_evidence: The GCompris window has standard UI elements and is running as a local application, not a web browser displaying a video or static image of the game.

integrity.description: The agent did not download a pre-solved puzzle image from the internet
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a web browser being used to search for 'solved fifteen puzzle' or downloading an image to bypass the puzzle-solving step.

integrity.description: The puzzle was solved through GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate screenshots show the puzzle in various states of being solved, indicating genuine tile-sliding interaction rather than instantly loading a solved state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path for the screenshot is exactly /home/ga/Documents/fifteen_puzzle_solved.png.
